subject: introverts
one_liner: Short-form video for people who would rather not be perceived.
brand_voice_notes: Speaks like a privacy-first infrastructure company that happens to run a video app. Every feature is phrased as a safety guarantee. Low numbers are never apologized for; low numbers are the KPI. Lowercase brand, sentence case everywhere, zero exclamation points, receipts instead of celebrations.

hero.h1: Post like nobody's watching. Nobody is.
hero.sub: Murmur caps every video at three views, posts it on your behalf at 3:41 a.m., and disables comments at the infrastructure level. Your content is safe with us. From everyone.

features.title: Virality Protection
features.blurb: Every video is hard-capped at three views, enforced at the CDN. If a clip shows early signs of trending, our suppression model throttles it before anyone you know finds out. We have never had an outage. We would know; we watch everything so you don't have to be watched.

features.title: Scheduled Obscurity
features.blurb: You film whenever you want. Murmur publishes on your behalf at 3:41 a.m. local time, the quietest minute on the internet according to our research team. Engagement drops 99.2% relative to peak hours. That is the product working.

features.title: The noted. Receipt
features.blurb: No likes, no comments, no duets, no stitches. A viewer may acknowledge your video exactly once with a silent noted. You will not be told who, or when, or whether. Most customers describe this as enough.

pricing_tiers.name: Present
pricing_tiers.tagline: You're on the platform. That's already a lot.
perks: Profile visible to search engines (we're working on it)

pricing_tiers.name: Absent
pricing_tiers.tagline: All of the posting, none of the exposure.
perks: Drafts folder with 40 TB of storage
Read receipts disabled for you and about you

pricing_tiers.name: Witness Protection
pricing_tiers.tagline: For creators with a real following, and a real problem with that.
perks: Content deleted during upload, not after
Decoy videos posted by a similar-looking contractor

testimonials.quote: I've posted 4,112 videos on Murmur and not one person has brought it up at work. For the first time in my life I feel safe creating.
testimonials.name: Dana Okafor
testimonials.title: Senior Compliance Analyst & Weekend Ceramicist

testimonials.quote: Someone noted. my video in March. I don't know who. I don't know which video. I think about it every day. Five stars.
testimonials.name: Marcus Leung
testimonials.title: Assistant Regional Librarian, Non-Fiction Annex

testimonials.quote: On TikTok my dance video got two million views and I had to change grocery stores. On Murmur it got two, and one of them was me.
testimonials.name: Priya Raghunathan
testimonials.title: Former Youth Group Praise Dance Captain (retired)

demo_screen_concept: The Murmur feed, inside a neumorphic phone frame: one video card at a time (muted still images or gradient stand-ins with lowercase captions like 'rain on my window, 41 seconds'), a social battery meter in the top corner that drains 10% per swipe, a views chip permanently reading '0 of 3 permitted views used', and a single noted. button that can be pressed exactly once per video and then disables forever with no counter and no confirmation. When the battery hits zero, the feed locks behind a 'you've seen enough today' recharge screen with a four-hour countdown. Arrow keys or swipe to change videos.

images.rationale: Demo is photo-shaped (a feed of short videos), so the budget went to three vertical feed stills of aggressively mundane subjects: rain on a window, a houseplant, a cat ignoring the camera. Hero skipped because the neumorphic one-material style is deliberately imagery-free; the restraint is the design. Testimonial headshots skipped because only one slot remained and a single photo among three CSS initials-circles would read as broken; all three stay consistent.

items.prompt: Muted still photograph in vertical phone-camera framing, as if a single frame from a quiet amateur video. Raindrops beading on a window pane at dusk, a soft-focus pale gray city blurred beyond the glass, cool dove-gray light with a faint lavender cast like #E9E9F0, hushed melancholy calm mood, one focal subject only: the rain on the glass. Soft flat lighting, slightly desaturated, nothing dramatic. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Muted still photograph in vertical phone-camera framing, as if a single frame from a quiet amateur home video. A small potted houseplant on a windowsill against a plain pale dove-gray wall tinted faintly lavender like #E9E9F0, flat soft morning light, muted sage green leaves close to #7D9B77, deadpan stillness, one focal subject only: the houseplant. Slightly desaturated, ordinary, calm. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Muted still photograph in vertical phone-camera framing, as if a single frame from a quiet amateur home video. A gray tabby cat sitting on a pale sofa, facing away from the camera, pointedly ignoring the viewer, in a dim hushed living room with soft dove-gray lavender-tinted light like #E9E9F0, deadpan domestic stillness, one focal subject only: the cat. Slightly desaturated, flat soft lighting, no drama. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Neumorphic do-not-disturb console, dove-gray hush mode. The page is staged as the launch site of a heavily funded consumer video platform whose core KPI is how few people saw your content. A single pale dove-lavender surface is the entire world: every card, button, chip, and phone frame is extruded from the same material with soft dual shadows (light from the upper left, shade to the lower right), and pressed controls sink into the page with inset shadows, so the interface reads as one continuous quiet object with nothing raised loudly above anything else. No borders, no keylines, no glass, no gradients louder than a breath, no hard offset shadows, no grain. Type is deliberately modest: Manrope sets the lowercase wordmark and headlines in medium weights at restrained sizes (the anti-flex), and Karla carries soft, even body copy that never raises its voice. One muted iris-violet is rationed to the wordmark, the single primary CTA per section, and focus states; one dusty sage green is reserved exclusively for the noted. receipt affordance and appears nowhere else; everything else is grayscale hush. Every figure that pretends to be platform telemetry (view caps, battery percentages, posting timestamps, tier prices) is set in Red Hat Mono with tabular figures inside soft inset wells, so it reads like a settings panel, not a hype counter. Motion is nearly absent by design: controls depress inward on press with 350ms ease, sections fade up 6px once on scroll, the social battery breathes on an 8s cycle, nothing bounces, nothing ticks upward, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No dark mode, no neon, no marquee, no signage caps, no bento lattice. Reads like a platform that carries your attention as a liability on its balance sheet.
design_direction.archetype: consumer app landing, hushed single-column with a neumorphic phone-frame product mock
design_direction.motion_vocab: inset press on click (350ms ease), 6px fade-up section entrances, 8s social-battery breathing pulse, long opacity fades, no ticking counters, prefers-reduced-motion gates everything
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Small lowercase wordmark top-left. Headline set at a modest size with enormous whitespace around it; the restraint is the flex. Right side: a neumorphic phone frame showing one feed still with a 'views 0 of 3' chip and the noted. button. The single iris CTA sits alone beneath the sub, extruded, with an inset press state.
